title: Show Items with an Active Timeline in My Week (not just when the timeline ends)
excerpt: As perfectly stated in the following FAQ question, I would like to ability to see an item in the My Week view which has a Timeline column (rather than a date column). Currently, the item only appears in the My Week view …

posts.author: mpmcqueen (Matt McQueen)
posts.cookedHtml:
As perfectly stated in the following FAQ question, I would like to ability to see an item in the My Week view which has a Timeline column (rather than a date column). Currently, the item only appears in the My Week view on the final date of the Timeline. I think this item should appear in the My Week view through every day that the Timeline extends. This allows me to see, at a glance, what items are currently in progress

Active Timeline, in My Week, for a task due at a later week Platform discussions
Say I have a pulse who’s Timeline is 12/17 - 1/28 with a due date of 1/28. Today is 12/17 and I want this pulse to appear in My Week as I know I should be working on this (given the active timeline), even though the due date is 1/28. Is there a way to make this appear in My Week?

posts.author: Eltjo (Eltjo Smit)
posts.cookedHtml: Timeline in My week is a perfect match! This would totally empower the My week overview section. Great idea.

posts.author: mattstockwell (Matt Stockwell)
posts.cookedHtml: To be honest, the ‘my week’ agenda is useless the way it is because if any of your task/pulse timelines are more than a single day, ‘my week’ doesn’t tell you they need working on (apart from on the last day)! I tend to leave things to the last minute, but scheduling software that encourages this is ridiculous!

posts.author: Sotiris (bootstraps)
posts.cookedHtml:
any updates? We use monday on tablets in our workshop.
But its verry hard for employees to know what to work on as they can not see the tasks in my week even if the timeline is in the week…

posts.author: stefancroweult (Stefan)
posts.cookedHtml: This makes no sense how it currently works - going by end date means tasks that are currently in progress (e.g. start date has passed) but not due to finish for some time (e.g. a few weeks) are buried all the way down the list even though technically they are active “today”.
